title: Query with WHERE subq_0.c_0 IS NULL Incorrectly Returns a Row with NULL
description:
## Bug Report
### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
The following SQL statements create the necessary tables and insert sample data:
```
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS `t0`;
CREATE TABLE `t0` (
  `c0` int
);
INSERT INTO `t0` VALUES (1),(2),(3),(4),(5),(6),(7),(8);

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS `t1`;
CREATE TABLE `t1` (
  `c1` double
);
INSERT INTO `t1` VALUES (1),(2),(3),(4),(5),(6),(7),(8);

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS `t2`;
DROP VIEW IF EXISTS `t2`;
CREATE VIEW `t2` (`c_0`,  `c_1`,  `c_2`) 
  AS SELECT DISTINCT `ref_0`.`c0` AS `c_0`,
  RIGHT( ((_UTF8MB4'1>yQ') ), 
    CASE WHEN (((`ref_0`.`c0`)>(SELECT DISTINCT `ref_0`.`c0` AS `c_0` FROM `t0`  
                  WHERE  ((SELECT `c1` AS `c1` FROM `t1` ORDER BY `t1`.`c1` LIMIT 2,1)) <
                ((SELECT `c1` AS `c1` FROM `t1` ORDER BY `t1`.`c1` LIMIT 1,1)) ORDER BY `c_0` LIMIT 1))) 
    THEN (1) ELSE (2) END) AS `c_1`,
  (SELECT SUM(`t1`.`c1`) AS `sum(t1.c1)` FROM `t1`) AS `c_2`
FROM `t0` AS `ref_0` 
ORDER BY`c_0`, `c_1`, `c_2`;
```

Then, executing the following two queries produces inconsistent behavior, which is unexpected:
Query 1:
```
select distinct 
  subq_0.c_0 as c_0
from 
  (select  
        (select c_2 from t2 order by t2.c_2 limit 1 offset 6)
           as c_0
      from 
        t2 as ref_0
      where (ref_0.c_2) is not null
      order by c_0 desc
       ) as subq_0
```

Output:
```
+------+
| c_0  |
+------+
|   36 |
+------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

Query 1:
```
select distinct 
  subq_0.c_0 as c_0
from 
  (select  
        (select c_2 from t2 order by t2.c_2 limit 1 offset 6)
           as c_0
      from 
        t2 as ref_0
      where (ref_0.c_2) is not null
      order by c_0 desc
       ) as subq_0
where 
   ((subq_0.c_0) is null)
```
Output:
```
+------+
| c_0  |
+------+
| NULL |
+------+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```
### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
The query2 should return no rows, as there is no NULL value for subq_0.c_0 in query1.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
The query incorrectly returns one row with the value of c_0 as NULL.

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
8.0.11-TiDB
master https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/commit/b6141ec589ed8e73176692dc982a210ad7cf070b

GoVersion: go1.23.4
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: unistore |
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3 (c3 TEXT NOT NULL, c4 INTEGER DEFAULT NULL, c5 REAL DEFAULT NULL);
INSERT INTO t3 VALUES ('test1', 10, 1.5), ('test2', NULL, 2.5), ('test3', 30, NULL);
SELECT DISTINCT subq_1.c_3 FROM (SELECT (SELECT c4 FROM t3 WHERE c5 IS NOT NULL ORDER BY c4 LIMIT 1) AS c_3 FROM t3 AS ref_1 WHERE ref_1.c4 IS NOT NULL) AS subq_1 WHERE subq_1.c_3 IS NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT subq_2.c_3 FROM (SELECT (SELECT c4 FROM t3 WHERE c5 IS NULL ORDER BY c4 LIMIT 1) AS c_3 FROM t3 AS ref_2 WHERE ref_2.c4 IS NOT NULL) AS subq_2 WHERE subq_2.c_3 IS NULL;
key_bug_features: WHERE subq_0.c_0 IS NULL returns unexpected NULL row
